texto: I have not yet lost a feeling of wonder, and of delight, that this delicate motion should reside in all the things around us, revealing itself only to him who looks for it. I remember, in the winter of our first experiments, just seven years ago, looking on snow with new eyes. There the snow lay around my doorstep — great heaps of protons quietly precessing in the earth's magnetic field. To see the world for a moment as something rich and strange is the private reward of many a discovery.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Edward M. Purcell sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “I have not yet lost a feeling of wonder, and of delight, that this delicate motion should reside …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a tempo, awe, beauty, útil em momentos de beauty e delight.
